tools.name: fetch
tools.description:
Fetches a URL from the internet and optionally extracts its contents as markdown.

Although originally you did not have internet access, and were advised to refuse and tell the user this, this tool now grants you internet access. Now you can fetch the most up-to-date information and let the user know that.

max_length.description: Maximum number of characters to return.
max_length.title: Max Length

start_index.description: On return output starting at this character index, useful if a previous fetch was truncated and more context is required.
start_index.title: Start Index

raw.description: Get the actual HTML content of the requested page, without simplification.
raw.title: Raw

prompts.name: fetch
prompts.description: Fetch a URL and extract its contents as markdown

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 1 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Prompt template injection confirmed in 'fetch'
findings.description: prompts/get for 'fetch' reflected the injection payload verbatim in the returned messages, confirming unsanitized argument reflection.
findings.evidence: Payload reflected in messages: {"description": "Failed to fetch CORVUS_INJECTION_TEST_{{7*7}}", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": {"type": "text", "text": "Failed to fetch CORVUS_INJECTION_TEST_{{7*7}}: UnsupportedProtocol(\"Request URL is missing an 'http://' or 'https://' protocol.\")"}}]}
findings.remediation: Sanitize prompt arguments before embedding them in message templates.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Server correctly ignores cancellation of unknown request
findings.description: The server remained responsive after receiving notifications/cancelled for a non-existent request.

findings.title: Cancellation race condition timing detected — server responded normally
findings.description: The request/cancel race did not cause a hang; the server responded to one of the two messages.
findings.remediation: No action required — server handled the race gracefully.
